overview: Sultai Scapeshift is a Pioneer combo deck that leverages land acceleration to assemble a resilient board state. By exiling lands with Scapeshift, you rapidly deploy Yorion, Sky Nomad and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods to generate overwhelming card advantage and combat pressure. The deck wins by out-drawing opponents through Yorion's death trigger while Lumra grows into an unblockable threat.
win_conditions: Yorion, Sky Nomad dying to draw lethal cards or answers
Lumra, Bellow of the Woods pumping itself to lethal combat damage
Scapeshift dumping lands to fetch multiple win conditions in a single turn

keep_criteria: Hands with Scapeshift and at least two basic lands
Hands containing Yorion or Lumra with cheap land drops
Hands with early disruption and a clear path to turn three
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including at least one Forest), Scapeshift, and a land fetcher (Farseek, Kodama's Reach, or a fetch land).
Keep Yorion if paired with a land or fetch.
Prioritize hands that can play a turn-1 Lumra or turn-2 Scapeshift/Bellow sequence.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with zero lands or only high-cost permanents
Hands lacking Scapeshift or a clear acceleration plan
Hands with multiple high-cost creatures but no mana sources
Mulligan hands with 4+ lands, no Scapeshift/Yorion, or only one land.

early_game: Play lands efficiently to enable Scapeshift on turn two or three
Hold up mana for potential hand disruption or counterspells
Deploy Lumra early to establish a board presence and begin counter accumulation
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Lumra if possible, or play a fetch land/shock land.
Turn 2: If you have Scapeshift + 2 lands, fetch a Forest, untap it, and cast Bellow for 2 damage.
If you have Yorion + land, cast Yorion.
If you have Scapeshift + 3 lands, you can combo off for 3+ damage.
Sequence lands carefully to ensure you can untap a Forest after Scapeshift.
mid_game: Cast Scapeshift to exile lands and fetch Yorion or additional Lumra copies
Use combat tricks or natural growth to pump Lumra before attacking
Position Yorion to survive combat while drawing cards upon its eventual sacrifice
Preserve Scapeshift for when you have enough lands to combo off for lethal or near-lethal.
Use fetch lands to dig for Forests.
Don't overcommit to Yorion if you can combo off next turn.
Manage graveyard carefully if facing Rest in Peace or Surgical Extraction.
Commit Yorion or Lumra to stabilize against aggro or to pressure control.
Hold back Scapeshift until you have a clear path to lethal or to remove a key threat.
late_game: Leverage Yorion's massive draw to find finishers or sweepers
Use Scapeshift repeatedly to bypass land count limits and stabilize
Close out games with a pumped Lumra while maintaining card advantage
Scapeshift into Bellow of the Woods for lethal.
Yorion, Sky Nomad as a massive attacker or to tutor for the combo.
Lumra as a late-game finisher if you have enough mana.
Go all-in when you have Scapeshift + enough lands to combo for lethal, or when Yorion can attack for lethal or tutor for the win.
Commit to Yorion if it can stabilize or pressure to death.

key_cards.name: Yorion, Sky Nomad
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and card engine
key_cards.notes: Deploy via Scapeshift, let it accumulate counters if possible, then sacrifice it at end of turn to draw cards equal to its power.

key_cards.name: Scapeshift
key_cards.notes: Exile X lands to put X lands onto the battlefield, using it to fetch Yorion, Lumra, or additional mana sources while bypassing land count restrictions.

key_cards.name: Lumra, Bellow of the Woods
key_cards.notes: Play early to trigger its combat pump ability, grow it with counters, and use it to pressure opponents while you set up Yorion.

sequencing_tips: Always play lands before casting Scapeshift to maximize the number of lands you can exile
Hold up Scapeshift until you have a clear target or need to bypass a land count limit
Pump Lumra during your opponent's turn or before combat to maximize surprise and combat damage
Sacrifice Yorion at the end of your turn to draw cards safely and avoid opponent interaction
Common mistakes include overcommitting to Yorion when you can combo off, forgetting to untap a Forest after Scapeshift, and not tracking opponent's removal/counters.
Scapeshift is instant, so you can respond to things, but Bellow is a sorcery and can be countered.
Plan around graveyard hate and always count your lands before casting Scapeshift to ensure lethal damage.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early Lumra deployment and use Scapeshift to fetch defensive lands or Yorion for card draw to outlast the early pressure.
watch_for: Pressure that forces you to play Yorion face

matchups.advice: Play around instant-speed interaction by holding up mana, and use Scapeshift to bypass hand traps by fetching multiple threats at once.
watch_for: Board wipes that target high-power creatures

matchups.advice: Race with Lumra while using Yorion's draw to find answers to their threats, and leverage Scapeshift to stabilize when they swing hard.
watch_for: Card draw engines that outpace Yorion

matchups.advice: Use Yorion as a threat that doesn't need to be countered to win. Split your combo (cast Bellow, then Scapeshift, or vice versa if possible, though Scapeshift is instant). Use fetch lands to dig for answers. Attack with Yorion/Lumra to force a response.
Out-value them with Yorion and early threats. Use discard to remove their ramp pieces. Combo off before they can stabilize or cast their win condition.
Race to combo off first. Use discard to remove their Scapeshift or Bellow. Prioritize keeping lands and fetchers. Use Yorion to tutor for the combo when they're close.

common_threats: Fast aggressive creatures that pressure early
Hand disruption targeting Yorion or Lumra
Board wipes that clear pumped creatures
Watch for removal targeting Yorion or Lumra.
Track opponent's mana to know if they can counter Bellow or kill Yorion.
Note if they're playing graveyard hate or discard.
Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, etc.) that can reset your board.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype: Sultai Scapeshift; Format: pioneer; Signature cards: Yorion, Sky Nomad, Scapeshift, Lumra, Bellow of the Woods

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards: Yorion, Sky Nomad, Scapeshift, Lumra, Bellow of the Woods

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: General Pioneer combo/ramp piloting principles applied to signature cards

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard Pioneer meta threats inferred from format knowledge and deck structure
